title: fix podman version output to include git commit and builttime
body:
Add the go module version v2 to the libpod path.

Fixes #7359

comments.body: and circling back a bit, an added test to make sure the version isn't at least blank would be welcomed.

Test suggestion:
```diff
index b23107e79..a5a3324fb 100644
--- a/test/system/001-basic.bats
+++ b/test/system/001-basic.bats
@@ -24,6 +24,13 @@ function setup() {
     is "${lines[0]}" "Version:[ ]\+[1-9][0-9.]\+" "Version line 1"
     is "$output" ".*Go Version: \+"               "'Go Version' in output"
     is "$output" ".*API Version: \+"             "API version in output"
+
+    # Test that build date is reasonable, e.g. after 2019-01-01
+    local built=$(expr "$output" : ".*Built: \+\(.*\)" | head -n1)
+    local built_t=$(date --date="$built" +%s)
+    if [ $built_t -lt 1546300800 ]; then
+        die "Preposterous 'Built' time in podman version: '$built'"
+    fi
 }


```

/approve
/lgtm
/hold
You can cancel hold once tests pass.

I've never seen that one before, but am going to restart anyway since it strikes me as unlikely that it's related to this PR.

The `fedora-31 RCLI:false` one is *literally* a flake I just fixed and got merged a few minutes ago, the `image trust` one. Restarting that too.

Failing [in bodhi](https://jenkins-continuous-infra.apps.ci.centos.org/blue/organizations/jenkins/fedora-rawhide-build-pipeline/detail/fedora-rawhide-build-pipeline/15060/pipeline/#step-177-log-805). That is, even though this PR had the desired effect in CI and in my test build on my laptop, it is failing in koji. @Luap99 any chance you could take a quick look for obvious problems? Link to [koji logs](https://koji.fedoraproject.org/koji/buildinfo?buildID=1595053)
